We are committed to ensure that the unique linguistic, cultural and social identity of the North Eastern States is adequately protected.
We will legislate a bill to prohibit and eliminate practices such as Triple Talaq and Nikah Halala.
We will expand forensic facilities and fast track courts to bring convicts to justice in cases of rape.
The BJP is committed to 33% reservation in parliament and state assemblies through a constitutional amendment.
We are committed to the empowerment and ‘development with dignity’ of all minorities.
We will prioritize Divyang beneficiaries under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana and mandate accessibility benchmarks to be met.
We will offer higher interest rates on fixed deposits by divyangs.
We will recognise the 11 left out Indian Gorkha sub-tribes as Schedule Tribes.

We will create an umbrella scheme ‘Pradhan Mantri Kalanidhi Yojana’ to allow flexible and customized packages of credit, working capital and social security options to provide comprehensive support to traditional arts.
We are committed to bring transgenders to the mainstream through adequate socio-economic and policy initiatives.
We are committed to conserve and promote all culturally, religiously and spiritually significant heritage sites related to every faith in India and the ongoing PRASAD scheme will be expanded.
We will constitute a National Task Force to study the status of all written and spoken languages and dialects in India.
We will also work towards revival and promotion of vulnerable or extinct dialects and languages.
We will ensure that the teaching of Sanskrit is expanded and popularised at the school level.
We will also provide 100 Panini fellowships to researchers and scholars across India to promote research in Sanskrit.
We will undertake every effort to ensure that the subject of faith, tradition and worship rituals related to Sabarimala are presented in a comprehensive manner before the Hon’ble Supreme Court.
We will endeavour to secure constitutional protection on issues related to faith and belief.
An International Cultural Festival will be organised on a grand scale every year in 5 different states to showcase and promote the rich diversity of Indian culture.
We will launch a web-enabled virtual tour of all culturally and historically significant heritage sites and museums in India through ‘Dharohar Dharshan-an integrated web portal’.
The BJP reiterates its stand to draft a Uniform Civil Code, drawing upon the best traditions and harmonizing them with the modern times.
We will create an institutional mechanism to deepen the relationship of culture and heritage with people of Indian origin.
Ensure justice for Muslim women by enacting the law against triple talaq.
To cater to the needs of big cities we will set up a mechanism of direct marketing of vegetables, fruits, dairy and fishery products through farmers’ cooperative organizations.
We will create 100 Innovation Zones in Urban Local Bodies.
We will launch a National Urban Mobility Mission to provide technology based urban mobility solutions to all urban local bodies.
Under this mission, we will incentivize cities to integrate the public transport systems.
We will also promote a common mobility card/ ticketing across different modes of transport.
In the next five years, we will ensure that 50 cities are covered with a strong metro network.
We will set up five regional centres of excellence on urban issues.
We will focus on fully developing the potential of tourism in these islands.
Ensure a pucca house to every family.
Complete the development of all projects under Swadesh Darshan, PRASAD and HRIDAY schemes.
We are committed to continue to build on the gains of 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' to provide accessible and affordable quality education to all women.
We will ensure that ample financial support is available to girls through out their education and subsidised education loans are provided for higher education.
To generate better work opportunities for women, 10% material to be sourced for government procurement will be done from MSMEs having at least 50% women employees in their workforce.
We will now strengthen the creche programme with special focus on the needs of parents employed in the unorganized sector.
We will ensure that the number of childcare facilities is increased three fold by 2022.
We will formulate a comprehensive ‘Women in Workforce’ roadmap focussed on dramatically increasing the female workforce participation rate over the next five years.
We will make every effort to ensure access to credit and other resources, capacity building, market and distribution network for women entrepreneurs, SHGs and women farmers.
We will focus on ensuring good quality, easily accessible and affordable maternal healthcare services for all women.
We will ensure that all reproductive and menstrual health services are easily available to all women across India.
With the expansion of ongoing Suvidha scheme, sanitary pads at an affordable cost of just Rs. 1 will be provided to all women and girls.
Gender sensitisation courses will be made an essential part of all educational institution’s curriculum and training modules of public offices.
Anganwadi and pre-school system will be strengthened to ensure that cases of disabilities are detected at an early stage.
We will formulate a comprehensive Child Protection Framework to facilitate the setting up of standards and robust inspection and monitoring of child care institutions across India.
We will undertake a rapid expansion of Yoga health hubs, Yoga tourism and research in Yoga.
Work towards increasing female workforce participation rate.
We would achieve more than 150 Technology Centres all over the country by 2024.

We will launch a new Science Mission for development of cutting edge technologies and future technologies with two submissions, specifically focused on (a) artificial intelligence mission and (b) robotic research mission.
We will start the 'National Program for Rapid Research in Socially Contextual Technologies' to create research paths in desirable technologies.
We will launch a special awareness and treatment programme for de-addiction among youth.
We will support a large scale internship programme for the youth in urban local bodies.
We will incentivise and reward self-organized groups of youth who adopt social assets like schools, hospitals, lakes, public gardens etc.
We will continue to provide adequate resources under the 'Khelo India' scheme.
Under this scheme, special attention will be given to encourage sporting talent among women and tribals.
We will map state and district-wise sport talent and strengths, recognize the traditional sporting practices and focus on the promotion of specific sports in specific regions.
We will introduce practical sports courses as part of the educational curriculum.
We will establish a National Sports Education Board for the development of sportspersons.
We will support the creation of a mini-stadium in each sub-district, with the participation of state governments.
We will formulate a mechanism to ensure that the capacity of existing sporting infrastructure is easily accessible for use of sportspersons and new models including PPP will be explored.
We will promote the Fit India campaign as an integral part of building a culture of sports in India.
We will ensure self-employment and skill development avenues for transgender youth.
We will further continue our efforts to promote Yoga globally.
Establish at least one Atal Tinkering Lab in every block.
We will present a separate Kisan Budget.
We will establish a permanent National Commission on Agricultural Development and Planning.
We will redesign the crop insurance scheme and direct insurance companies to charge premiums on a ‘no profit, no loss’ basis.
We will repeal the APMC Act and make trade in agricultural produce free from all restrictions.
We will formulate a policy on the export and import of agricultural products to support farmers and enhance their income.
We will promote organic farming, encourage mixed fertilizers and pesticides, and support verification of organic products.
We will double funding in 5 years for agricultural teaching, R&D, and applied sciences.
We will work tirelessly to assure food security to all.
We will increase defence spending to meet the requirements of the Armed Forces.
We will expedite all modernisation programmes of the armed forces transparently.
We will take strategic and hard measures to defend the territorial integrity of India and ensure the safety of our people.
We will evolve suitable policies to address data, cyber, financial, communication, and trade route security.
We will establish the office of Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) as the principal adviser to the Government on defence.
We will provide a statutory basis to the National Security Council and the office of the National Security Adviser.
We will re-establish the National Security Advisory Board (NSAB) with a statutory basis and appoint experts from different disciplines.
We will rapidly expand domestic capacity in the public and pre-qualified private sectors to manufacture defence and security equipment.
We will set up the National Counter-Terrorism Centre (NCTC) within 3 months.
We will make NATGRID operational by December 2019.
We will take an uncompromising approach and hard measures to end terrorism and militant infiltration.
We will fight violent Maoist activities while addressing developmental challenges and winning over cadres.
We will work with State Governments to increase the strength of State police forces, fill vacancies, and equip and train them.
We will increase the strength of border guarding forces (BSF, SSB, ITBP, Assam Rifles) and post them on or close to the border.
We will construct modern, well-equipped Integrated Border Outposts and reduce the distance between them.
We will reduce the posting period in high altitude border outposts to 3 months at a time.
We will enhance the capacity of the Border Roads Organisation and create separate divisions for the India-China and India-Myanmar borders.
We will remove the anomalies in the implementation of One Rank One Pension (OROP).
We will formulate a policy of compensation for the families of martyrs, including full pay, funding children's education, a government job, and monetary compensation.
We will allow Armed Forces personnel retiring after colour service lateral entry into the civil services based on qualifications.
We will allow Armed Forces personnel retiring before age 40 to enter the Central Armed Police Forces at suitable levels.
We will substantially expand the capacity of Armed Forces medical corps and hospitals for ex-servicemen and their families.
We will revise the disability determination procedure for injured armed forces personnel and withdraw all pending appeals filed by the Ministry of Defence.
We will increase the strength of the Central Armed Police Forces to meet growing border and internal security requirements.
We will ensure an adequate number of CAPF battalions are on rest, recuperation, and training at any given time.
We will enhance the status of CAPF jawans and benefits to their families in cases of death in action.
We will work with State Governments to augment police force sizes and fill current vacancies within 18 months.
We will review the deployment of armed forces, move more troops to the border, and reduce the presence of the Army and CAPFs in the Kashmir Valley.
We will strengthen the security of the NES borders with Bangladesh, Myanmar, and China, and resolve border issues through dialogue.
We will create an Enterprise Support Agency to help entrepreneurs with business support, incubation, access to technology, funding, and markets.
We will reward businesses that create new jobs by lowering effective direct tax rates and CSR fund contributions.
We will reward export-oriented industries through tax rebates and incentives.
We will establish an adequately capitalised Tourism Development Bank to provide low-cost, long-term funds for tourism-related businesses.
We will offer lower rates of corporate and personal income tax on tourism-related business income.
We will increase the share of India’s manufacturing sector to 25 per cent of GDP within 5 years.
We will announce policies to help India retain its leading position in manufacturing engineering goods, petroleum products, gems & jewellery, pharmaceuticals, garments & textiles, and chemicals.
We will announce policies to help India gain a leadership position in steel, metals, cement, machinery, electronic goods, computer hardware, and automobiles.
We will announce a ‘Make for the World’ policy inviting companies to invest in Exclusive Export-only Zones.
We will revive stalled projects to bring locked-up capital to use and generate employment.
We will acquire patents, create a patent pool, and make advanced technologies available to small and medium businesses.
We will review and repeal unnecessary rules and regulations made in the last 5 years to free industry and business.
We will review discretionary powers of tax authorities and investigative agencies to allow industry and business to function with freedom.
We will replace the Essential Commodities Act, 1955 with an enabling law invocable only in emergencies.
We will achieve the fiscal deficit target of 3 per cent of GDP by 2020-21 and contain the revenue deficit under 1 per cent of GDP.
We will reverse unwarranted interference into the functioning of the RBI and respect its autonomy.
We will provide a conducive policy climate to recognise the contributions of the private and public sectors and promote entrepreneurship.
We will extend full support to the private sector to revive entrepreneurship.
We will create a policy environment to encourage domestic consumption.
We will adopt a foreign trade policy to ensure exports become a key driver of economic growth.
We will encourage household saving to achieve a savings level of 40 per cent of GDP and a Gross Capital Formation level of 35 per cent of GDP.
We will work with banks and insurance companies to introduce simple financial products for households.
We will welcome FDI in all sectors, subject to national security exceptions.
We will intervene in markets only where absolutely necessary to prevent misallocation of resources.
We will review all laws, rules, and regulations governing investments within 3 months and repeal outdated ones.
We will review and restate the Foreign Trade Policy within 3 months.
We will work with the RBI to re-start credit delivery and ensure sufficient liquidity and cash in circulation.
We will enact and enforce a comprehensive Law on Doing Business in India.
We will endeavor to bring every Fortune 500 company to set up a business in India.
We will completely withdraw the Angel Tax imposed on Start-Ups.
We will devise a rehabilitation plan for MSMEs severely affected by demonetisation and GST.
We will disinvest from non-core, non-strategic central public sector enterprises.
We will induct good economic policy makers and managers into the administration and give them autonomy.
We will get the government out of gratuitous market interventions and address notable market failures through regulation.
We will transfer Rs. 72,000 a year to the poorest 20 per cent of families in India under the Nyuntam Aay Yojana (NYAY).
We will enact the Direct Taxes Code in the first year of our government.
We will levy taxes at reasonable and progressive rates under the Direct Taxes Code.
We will replace the current GST laws with a GST 2.0 regime.
We will implement a single, moderate, standard rate of tax on all goods and services under GST 2.0.
We will levy a special rate of duty on demerit goods under GST 2.0.
We will bring Real Estate, Petroleum Products, Tobacco, and Liquor within the ambit of GST 2.0.
We will exempt or zero-rate essential goods of mass consumption and essential services under GST 2.0.
We will zero-rate all exported goods and services under GST 2.0.
We will ensure threshold exemptions for small businesses are not affected by inter-state supply.
We will remove GST liability on purchasers through the reverse charge mechanism for supplies from unregistered businesses.
We will abolish the e-way bill.
We will require taxpayers to file a simple, single quarterly return and an annual return.
We will serve the GST Council with a permanent secretariat of tax experts.
We will ensure DTC and GST 2.0 are essentially civil laws with prosecution only for criminal conspiracy, corruption, or fraud.
We will comprehensively review the concept, role, and functions of Public Sector Banks.
We will amalgamate Public Sector Banks so that there are only 6-8 PSBs with a national presence.
We will ensure PSBs continue to lend to priority and disadvantaged sectors.
We will review and strengthen the regulatory regime of NBFCs.
We will develop a robust corporate bond market, municipal bond market, and infrastructure investment trusts.
We will promote new Development Banks to provide long-term credit to development and infrastructure projects.
We will set up an India Global Companies Fund to support Indian-owned technology companies to scale up globally.
We will encourage the establishment of State-level and Regional-level small banks to provide credit to MSMEs.
We will encourage State Governments to revive State Financial Corporations to provide long-term credit to MSMEs.
We will strengthen and technologically-enable Regional Rural Banks and Banking Correspondents to accelerate financial inclusion.
We will work with the RBI to simplify the KYC process and avoid repetitive verification.
We will develop a deep and broad securities market offering safe and adequate returns to investors.
We will adopt policies to attract more Indian and foreign investment in our financial markets.
We will take hard measures to prevent fraud in banking, securities, and financial markets and deliver swift punishment to offenders.
We will work with industry to increase expenditure on science and technology to 2 per cent of GDP.
We will strengthen patent laws and support Indian inventors to secure international patent protection.
We will create a patent pool and allow businesses access to patented technology at affordable costs.
We will revitalise the National Innovation Council to formulate and implement strategies for innovation.
We will establish a National Mission focused on sunrise technologies like Big Data, Machine Learning, IoT, and 3-D Printing.
We will introduce a special tax regime for tourism-related businesses and income.
We will design and promote an NRI Invest Scheme and establish a single point of contact for NRI investments.
We will implement the recommendations of the Fifteenth Finance Commission and urge States to implement their State Finance Commissions' recommendations.
We will enhance financial assistance to the Autonomous District Councils in the North Eastern States.
We will take bold measures to enhance trade, especially border trade, with neighbouring countries for the NES.
We will create lakhs of new jobs for teachers, doctors, nurses, paramedics, technicians, instructors and administrators by expanding the education and health sectors.
We will allocate sufficient funds to promote science, technology, and innovation.
We will strengthen Science Departments in NAAC-graded colleges and universities with funds for equipment, labs, and teachers.
We will set up schools on the model of Sainik schools for the children of CAPF personnel.
We will promote art education in schools and colleges and support institutions that exclusively teach the arts.
We will encourage scholars to study archaeology and anthropology by providing more scholarships and fellowships.
We will support the establishment of museums, art galleries, libraries, and archives and ensure they are accessible.
We will allocate funds to create digital archives of India’s art and culture.
We will review and expand higher education opportunities and review the fee structure for NRI children in India.
We will support State Governments in promoting the mother tongue as the preferred medium of instruction.
We will support State Governments in teaching the State's official language(s), Hindi, and English.
We will work with State Governments to impart education and skills to Denotified and Semi-Nomadic Tribes, especially children.
We will establish a National Centre of Research and Excellence for Special Education to ensure quality education for children with special needs.
We will propose transferring School Education to the State List in the Seventh Schedule while retaining Higher Education in the Union List.
We will make school education from Class I to Class XII in public schools compulsory and free by amending the RTE Act.
We will end the practice of charging special fees for different purposes in public schools.
We will attach the highest importance to learning outcomes to address poor education quality.
We will increase the capacity, number, and quality of government Teacher Training Institutes.
We will implement a scheme for the periodic and continuing education of teachers, making participation mandatory.
We will double the allocation for Education to 6 per cent of GDP in the 5 years ending 2023-24.
We will increase expenditure to provide basic and adequate infrastructure like classrooms, libraries, labs, playgrounds, toilets, and drinking water in every school.
We will increase the number of Kendriya Vidyalayas and Navodya Vidyalayas based on demand.
We will arrest the school dropout trend and ensure every child completes 12 years of school education.
We will employ technology-enabled teaching methods and personalised, adaptive learning tools in all schools.
We will introduce vocational training as a compulsory component of school education in Classes IX to XII.
We will support the establishment of special schools for children with special needs.
We will establish more public universities, especially in under-served areas.
We will restore the autonomy of colleges and universities.
We will entrust the Regulation, Grading, and Funding of colleges and universities to separate organisations.
We will include Guest, Temporary, and Contract Teachers in the regular establishment of Universities so they receive their due benefits.
We will restore the 200-point roster system for appointments to central universities and institutes of higher learning.
We will ensure serving teachers are represented in the governing bodies of colleges and universities.
We will establish more Institutes of Higher Learning in medicine, engineering, commerce, management, and pure sciences.
We will increase the Gross Enrollment Ratio in Higher Education to at least 40 in 5 years.
We will dispense with the NEET examination and substitute it with a State-level examination for admission to medical colleges.
We will encourage colleges and universities to award Deprivation Points to students based on gender, rural residence, or being a first-generation learner to ensure diversity.
We will increase the amount earmarked for grants to colleges and universities.
We will increase the number of scholarships offered to students and encourage institutions to build endowment funds.
We will revive, redesign, and expand the Education Loan programme with no interest charged during the period of study.
We will waive outstanding interest dues on old education loans as on 31 March 2019.
We will pass a Students Rights Bill to codify the rights and obligations of students in colleges and universities.
We will work with State Governments to ensure children receive good quality education reflected in learning outcomes.
We will launch a Water Bodies Restoration Mission to create jobs.
We will review, re-formulate, and implement the policy on spectrum and natural resource exploration and extraction.
We will formulate a policy on Clean Energy in existing fossil fuel power plants and promote Green Energy.
We will enhance electricity availability in rural areas by encouraging investment in off-grid renewable power generation.
We will ensure every village and every home is electrified in the true sense.
We aim to substitute LPG used in homes with electricity and solar energy in the long term.
We will encourage non-motorised transport like walking and cycling.
We will remove inconsistencies between the Forest Rights Act and the CAMPA Act and reaffirm the role of the Gram Sabha.
We will use modern technology to harness the waters of the Brahmaputra and find a permanent solution to floods and soil erosion.
We will create a Ministry of Water to bring all water-related activities and departments under one authority.
We will focus on water storage in dams and water bodies, replenishing ground water, and creating a large participatory programme of water management.
We will double the budget allocation for cleaning rivers, including the Ganga.
We will review the methodology for cleaning rivers, employ the latest science and technology, and convert the Ganga Action Plan into a People’s Programme.
We will place India at the forefront of the battle against global warming and defend India’s interests in international environmental negotiations.
We will constitute an independent, empowered, and transparent Environment Protection Authority (EPA) by law to establish and enforce environmental standards.
We will significantly strengthen the National Clean Air Programme to urgently tackle pollution by targeting and mitigating all major sources of emission.
We will formulate a comprehensive land and water use policy and plan for the conservation of ecosystems, bio-diversity, and wildlife.
We will work with State Governments to preserve the bio-diversity of the Himalayan range and the Western Ghats while protecting local livelihoods.
We will stop the discharge of effluents into rivers and clean all rivers of India.
We will protect coastal zones by reversing recent steps that diluted coastal zone regulations, without affecting fishing communities' livelihoods.
We will impose a complete ban on the import of all types of waste.
We will implement a solid waste management plan in every habitation using modern technology and assure appropriate equipment and safe working conditions for waste management workers.
We will confer legal and financial powers upon Panchayats and Municipalities to partner with State Governments in environment protection and management.
We will amend forest laws, redefine the role of Forest Departments, and make local communities the custodians and shareholders of forest resources.

We will work with State Governments to increase forest cover to 25 per cent by 2025 by re-defining 'forest', assessing exact cover, involving local communities in afforestation, and penalising the diversion of forest funds.
We will preserve wildlife habitats, minimise human-wildlife conflict, and award compensation for the loss of human lives.
We will provide clean cooking fuels at affordable prices to all households and monitor LPG cylinder prices to mitigate burdens through subsidies.
We will present national accounts accounting for the costs of environmental degradation and adopt Green Budgeting principles in the Annual Budget.
We will endeavour to make India a green manufacturing hub by reducing tariffs and lifting trade barriers for clean technology.
We will review the NDMA and emphasise the function of preventing disasters.
We will develop a modern Climate Information System incorporating advanced meteorology tools to monitor, forecast, and disseminate information to stakeholders.
We will increase the allocation to the National Adaptation Fund and re-visit guidelines to ensure only schemes benefiting farmers, fisherfolk, forest dwellers, and artisans are implemented.
We will establish a permanent mechanism with neighbouring countries to resolve problems faced by fisherfolk and eliminate conflict.
We will continue the policy of friendship, peaceful co-existence, non-alignment, and increased bilateral engagement.
We will establish a National Council on Foreign Policy consisting of cabinet members, scholars, experts, and diplomats.
We will work closely with all countries, particularly neighbours and G-20 countries, in multi-lateral forums.
We will make foreign trade an important element of foreign policy and significantly increase trade with other countries.
We will strengthen relations with other countries through enhanced economic cooperation and two-way investments.
We will work with other countries to eliminate terrorist groups, terrorist acts, and cross-border terrorism.
We will persuade other countries to compel Pakistan to verifiably end its support to terrorist groups.
We will pass a Law on Asylum consistent with international treaties and conventions.
We will re-double efforts to win permanent membership for India in the UN Security Council and the Nuclear Suppliers Group.
We will work with SAARC and ASEAN countries to enhance trade, investments, tourism, and cultural exchanges.
We will establish effective mechanisms with neighbouring countries to resolve problems faced by fishermen.
We will strengthen the Indian Council of Cultural Relations to project India’s soft power globally.
We will significantly increase the size of the Foreign Service, induct domain experts, and open more missions abroad.
We will make every effort to retrieve India’s stolen art.
We will task India’s Missions abroad with encouraging more visitors to India.
We will expand the Visa-on-Arrival Scheme and charge no fee for tourist visas for a period of 3 years.
We will re-establish the Ministry of Overseas Indians to address the concerns of NRIs.
We will task India’s Missions abroad to pay special attention to the work safety and conditions of Indian citizens in foreign countries.
We will appoint a Committee of Overseas Indian Citizens to review the work of Missions regarding NRIs.
We will take the path of dialogue to understand the aspirations of the people of Jammu & Kashmir and find an honourable solution.
We will adopt a two-pronged approach of uncompromising firmness on the border to end infiltration, and absolute fairness in dealing with the demands of the people.
We will find an innovative federal solution for J&K through patient dialogue and talks with all stakeholders.
We will offer talks without pre-conditions to the people of J&K and appoint 3 interlocutors from civil society.
We will address illegal immigration into the NES through consultation and evolve a consensus on legislative measures to secure indigenous identities.
We will resolve pending inter-state and boundary issues in the NES through dialogue or mediation.
We will abolish application fees for government examinations and posts.
We will introduce a new model of urban governance with a directly elected mayor with a fixed 5-year term and an elected Council.
We will enforce the 74th Amendment and ensure the devolution of powers, functions, and funds to Municipalities and Corporations.
We will ensure debt is treated as a civil liability and no criminal proceedings will be instituted against farmers unable to pay their debt.
We will faithfully report off-Budget and extra-Budget borrowings with justification.
We will require the RBI Governor to periodically render an account to a committee of Parliament.
We will build capacity in government for taxation and delivery of public goods and services.
We will appoint an independent panel of experts to oversee the design and implementation of NYAY.
We will allocate a share of GST revenues to Panchayats and Municipalities.
We will review the governance structure of PSBs and abolish the Banking Board Bureau.
We will set up a Scenario Planning and Strategic Futures Office to aid informed policy making.
We will hold the District Administration responsible for riots, communal violence, and widespread breakdown of law and order.
We will set up a Committee to draft new Service Rules for CAPF personnel.
We will grant autonomy, including financial autonomy, to cultural institutions.
We will re-constitute the National Integration Council to promote unity and fight divisive forces.
We will create an institutional framework for Central and State Governments to consult civil society organisations on policies and programmes.
We will use modern technology to increase the role of civil society organisations in policy and decision-making.
We will restore the dignity, authority, and autonomy of independent institutions and make them accountable to Parliament.
We will ensure transparent selection and appointment to independent bodies.
We will amend the Anti-Defection Law to provide for instant disqualification of MLAs/MPs for disobedience to the party whip or withdrawing allegiance.
We will introduce the requirement of public consultation before a Bill is introduced in Parliament.
We will enact a Social Accountability Act to subject all government schemes and programmes to social audit.
We will amend the Aadhaar Act to restrict its use to government subsidies, benefits, and services, and allow alternate instruments of identification.
We will ensure the Leader of the Opposition is a full-fledged member of the Lokpal selection committee.
We will enforce anti-corruption laws without discrimination.
We will investigate deals entered into by the previous government, including the Rafale deal.
We will investigate how scamsters were allowed to leave the country and take steps to bring them back.
We will place before the people annually a statement on the implementation of manifesto promises.
We will conduct and publish annual Social Audits of implemented programmes.
We will review the RTI Act to omit diluting provisions and add provisions to strengthen it.
We will appoint qualified persons from different sections of society as Information Commissioners.
We will introduce a Diversity Index to assess and ensure diversity in all government bodies and public sector enterprises.
We will pass an Anti-Discrimination Law to prohibit discrimination in the supply of public goods and services.
We will re-introduce and pass the Grievance Redressal Bill to provide for mandatory, time-bound grievance redressal.
We will make rules to operationalise the Whistle-Blowers Protection Act and ensure it is not weakened.
We will review the Seventh Schedule and build a consensus to transfer some legislative fields from the Concurrent List to the State List.
We will reduce the size of Central Government Ministries and Departments where States have built sufficient capacity.
We will give primacy to State Governments in school education, primary and secondary healthcare, child nutrition, drinking water, sanitation, and electricity distribution.
We will establish Councils for Agriculture, Education, and Healthcare on the model of the GST Council.
We will prevail upon States to devolve funds, functions, and functionaries to Panchayats and Municipalities.
We will empower Panchayats and Municipalities by devolving funds, including a share of GST revenues.
We will explore the possibility of direct central grants to Panchayats and Municipalities.
We will grant full statehood to Puducherry.
We will amend the GNCTD Act, 1991 to ensure the Lieutenant Governor acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers except on 3 reserved subjects.
We will establish a statutory Council for the UTs of Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Lakshadweep, Daman & Diu, and Dadra & Nagar Haveli to advise the Lieutenant Governor.
We will remove pre-qualifications like minimum education for candidates at elections to local bodies.
We will enhance the role of Gram Sabhas and make it obligatory for them to meet at least twice a year.
We will amend the Constitution to make the Supreme Court a Constitutional Court for cases involving constitutional interpretation and national importance.
We will amend the Constitution to establish a Court of Appeal between High Courts and the Supreme Court, sitting in 6 locations.
We will allocate sufficient funds annually for judiciary infrastructure.
We will fix the retirement age of High Court and Supreme Court judges, and judicial members in Tribunals, at 65 years.
We will work with the judiciary to introduce modern technology and appoint professionals for court and roster management.
We will encourage the judiciary to adopt technological tools to track cases and expedite hearings.
We will establish a National Judicial Commission comprising judges, jurists, and parliamentarians for the transparent selection of judges.
We will establish an independent Judicial Complaints Commission by law to investigate complaints of misconduct against judges.
We will decriminalise laws directed against civil violations and subject them to civil penalties.
We will omit Section 499 of the IPC and make defamation a civil offence.
We will omit Section 124A of the IPC (sedition).
We will amend laws allowing detention without trial to align with the Constitution and International Human Rights Conventions.
We will pass a Prevention of Torture Act to prohibit third-degree methods and punish police excesses.
We will amend AFSPA to balance security forces' powers with human rights and remove immunity for enforced disappearance, sexual violence, and torture.
We will amend laws to subject all investigation agencies to the same restrictions imposed on the police by the CrPC and Evidence Act.
We will amend the CrPC to affirm the principle that 'Bail is the rule and jail is the exception'.
We will immediately release undertrial prisoners facing charges punishable by up to 3 years imprisonment who have spent 3 months in prison.
We will immediately release undertrial prisoners facing charges punishable by 3 to 7 years imprisonment who have spent 6 months in prison.
We will institute comprehensive prison reforms recognizing prisoners' human and legal rights.
We will scrap the Electoral Bond Scheme.
We will set up a National Election Fund to allocate funds to recognised political parties based on criteria laid down by law.
We will ensure EVMs and VVPATs are tamperproof and match the count in at least 50 per cent of EVMs against VVPAT slips during counting.
We will allocate substantially more free airtime on All India Radio and Doordarshan to recognised political parties during elections.
We will consult State Governments to reach a consensus on police reforms and pass a Model Police Act.
We will make police forces modern, technology-enabled, people-friendly, and upholders of human rights through the Model Police Act.
We will make State police forces accountable to an independent Police Accountability Commission.
We will decentralise the police force and involve the community in its oversight.
We will mandate investigations into communal riots, lynchings, and gang rapes by a special wing of the State police under direct command of State Headquarters.
We will ensure State police forces reflect the diversity of the State's population and give greater representation to under-represented sections.
